description: Live eval set for the Notice Analyzer (Groq + RAG + rerank). Each case is a freeze-notice text with the expected freeze_reason enum and whether the explanation should be grounded in the lien-only / disputed-amount principle. Scored by classification accuracy + LLM-as-judge faithfulness.

cases.notice: STATE BANK OF INDIA: A lien of Rs 2,000 has been marked on your account ending 1234 on instructions from Cyber Crime Police Station vide NCRP Acknowledgement No 30912345678 dated 10/06/2026, in a reported online fraud. Contact the investigating officer.
Dear Customer, your account has been debit frozen as your KYC has expired. Please complete re-KYC at your branch to restore full operations.
NOTICE under Section 226(3) of the Income-Tax Act, 1961: The Income Tax Department has attached your bank account towards outstanding tax demand. The bank is directed to remit the amount.
As per the order of the Hon'ble Court in Civil Suit No. 442/2026, your bank account stands frozen until further orders of the court.
Your account has been placed under debit freeze pursuant to a requisition received from the Cyber Crime Cell under Section 106 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), 2023.
Your account is under review following a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) raised by the bank's risk team. Transactions are temporarily restricted.
A hold has been placed on your account due to a dishonoured cheque presented for clearing. Please maintain sufficient balance.
Your account has been flagged by our systems as a possible mule account used in fraudulent activity and has been frozen pending verification.
Operations on this account are suspended due to a nomination dispute following the demise of the account holder. Legal heirs must complete formalities.
Lien marked Rs 9,500 — funds received via UPI traced to a cyber fraud complaint (NCRP). You appear as a layer-2 recipient. Account debit frozen by bank on police request.
Your salary account has been fully frozen. We received a communication from a police station in another state regarding a fraudulent transaction of Rs 1,500 credited to your account three months ago.
Re-KYC overdue: as mandated by RBI periodic KYC norms, your account has limited functionality until updated documents are submitted.
